question_text: A student observes steam coming from a kettle spout. Which of the following best explains what is happening?

options.label: A
options.text: Water is boiling and the visible steam is water vapour that has cooled and condensed back to tiny water droplets

options.label: B
options.text: The water has turned into a gas that is visible in the air

options.label: C
options.text: The kettle is leaking hot liquid water into the air

options.label: D
options.text: Oxygen and hydrogen atoms are separating from the water molecules

explanation: True water vapour is invisible. The visible 'steam' is actually a cloud of tiny water droplets formed when hot invisible vapour cools slightly near the kettle spout and condenses back into a liquid.
